title: Status Lines, Informational Responses, Content Rules, and Response Parsing · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs status lines, informational responses, content rules, and response parsing from bounded byte and state evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the response control-data and content decision parser. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for response control-data and content decision parser after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse HTTP version, three-digit status code, optional reason phrase, and CRLF while treating the reason phrase as non-authoritative.
Process zero or more permitted informational responses before the final response with bounded count and protocol-specific handling.
Parse HEAD response metadata as if GET were selected while never consuming response content bytes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Status Line Parser
steps.instruction:
Order the Status Line Parser evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Status Line Parser.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Status Line Parser assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Status Line Parser, keep Status Line Parser connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A review protocol team handles case 9889 in a protocol upgrade gateway transferring buffered tunnel bytes. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound informational responses and transition HEAD, no-content, and CONNECT responses correctly; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then inject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and close before any downstream forwarding. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Parse HTTP version, three-digit status code, optional reason phrase, and CRLF while treating the reason phrase as non-authoritative.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Status Line Parser has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
